title: Sealed Packet I — Supplementary Interview Bundle

sections.heading: FOLLOW-UP NOTES
sections.body:
Charles Decroix, upon re-examination, amended his timeline. He now recalled that he had left the atelier not at eleven-forty but at twelve-twenty. He attributed the error to "a faulty memory for clocks." The amended statement does not agree with the door-book, which still shows eleven-forty. A porter at Decroix's building swears he saw Decroix return at twelve-thirty-five, which would require a twenty-minute walk from Sauval's atelier—possible, but tight. The witness refused to initial the correction. The desk clerk re-filed the discrepancy under ticket 39.

Set down under oath, and therefore no safer than any other vanity of men. The constables disputed the matter till dawn: whether the witness erred, dissembled, or sought by falsehood to shield a greater villainy. The margin is cleaner than the tale; that alone should give a sober mind pause. The desk-note beneath it read only: "keep apart from the louder papers."

A second observer, a gendarme who had been stationed outside the atelier for an unrelated matter, insisted that somebody—he would not name whom—had entered the building at seven-twenty and left at seven-forty-five, a window that overlaps Caillou's visit. This observer later recanted, saying he had confused the day. If one witness lied, they may have lied to protect themselves rather than the killer.

Marked reliable by the clerk who was himself twice found mistaken. It was observed at the desk that fear and deceit oft borrow one another's garments. Thus was it lodged in the file, though not one man at the desk would swear to the whole of it. The desk-note beneath it read only: "keep apart from the louder papers."

Pencilled upon the inward flap, as though it were but a warehouse remembrance: first bell, seventh stair, seventh lantern, seventh berth.

subtitle: Desk mark: doubtful. The same mark is set upon papers later proved sound.
